title: Cheirostylis chalmersii
intro: Cheirostylis chalmersii, also known as Chalmers' Liparis, is a species of orchid that belongs to the genus Cheirostylis. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Cheirostylis chalmersii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cheirostylis chalmersii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, particularly respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cheirostylis chalmersii is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. This species belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Goodyerinae. The genus Cheirostylis is part of the subtribe Goodyerinae within the tribe Goodyerinae. Cheirostylis chalmersii is further divided into one species, which is widely accepted. The classification of Cheirostylis chalmersii is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.
